expectations.text: The [!important] callout about TTL modes contains bullet points, not the original prose paragraph
The [!warning] about write-through + sliding TTL uses Pattern C structure: opening statement, detail bullets, bolded action item
expectations.evidence: Warning has bullets: True, has bold action item: True

expectations.text: Edge cases have expressive emoji (e.g., thundering herd, race condition, partial invalidation each get a distinct emoji)
expectations.evidence: Emoji near edge cases: {'thundering herd': [], 'race condition': [], 'partial invalidation': [], 'nested object': []} (0/4 have emoji)

expectations.text: Uses hierarchical (nested) bullet points — at least 3 instances of indented sub-bullets
No filler phrases like 'it is important to note', 'in conclusion', 'it is worth mentioning'
Contains at least 3 Obsidian-style callouts using '> [!type]' syntax
The three nested-object approaches (eager invalidation, version tags, short TTL) are presented in a structured format, not numbered prose paragraphs
Reference to docs/cache-benchmarks.md uses Tier 2 style (italic footnote below horizontal rule), not an inline link
